Import necessary modules: torch, numpy, transformers (RobertaConfig, RobertaModel), and any other dependencies needed for model setup and tensor operations
Set random seeds for reproducibility using torch.manual_seed(42) and numpy.random.seed(0)
Initialize a RobertaConfig with vocab_size=50265, pad_token_id=1, bos_token_id=0, eos_token_id=2, and other parameters as per the code context (e.g., hidden_size=256, num_hidden_layers=6)
Instantiate a RobertaModel with the above configuration
Create a dummy batch of data with batch_size=2, seq_len=10 (or any small fixed length), containing token indices that include the eos_token_id (2) at a known position, e.g., position 8 or 9
Construct the action_mask tensor for the batch with shape (batch_size, seq_len), setting 1 for valid tokens and 0 for padding tokens, ensuring that the eos token position is masked as valid (1)
Define start index as 1 or 2 (to simulate the start offset used in the bug report)
Generate dummy tensors for actor_log_prob, log_probs, and advantages with shapes compatible with (batch_size, seq_len), filled with arbitrary but consistent values (e.g., torch.randn or torch.ones)
Implement two variants of the mask slicing: one using action_mask[:, start:] (the buggy version) and another using action_mask[:, start-1:-1] (the proposed alternative)

Calculate actor loss using the actor_loss_fn function (can be mocked or use a simple loss function like mean squared error for reproduction) with inputs sliced accordingly (actor_log_prob[:, start:], log_probs[:, start:], advantages, action_mask sliced as per the buggy version)
Repeat the actor loss calculation using the alternative mask slicing (action_mask[:, start-1:-1]) with correspondingly sliced inputs
Print or log the mask slices, especially the tokens corresponding to the eos position, to verify if eos token is included or excluded in each mask variant
Assert that the eos token is excluded from the loss calculation mask in the buggy case (action_mask[:, start:]) and included in the alternative mask case (action_mask[:, start-1:-1])
Verify that the difference in mask slices leads to a difference in loss values, indicating that eos token’s contribution is filtered out in the buggy version but present in the alternative
Optionally, add assertions or printouts to confirm that the eos token has a non-zero reward or advantage value in the inputs, ensuring the masking impacts the loss meaningfully
Summarize the observations to confirm that using action_mask[:, start:] filters out the eos token loss while the alternative mask keeps it, reproducing the bug as reported
